text: The surgeon uses a robotic EndoWrist instrument on the right side and a bipolar Maryland on the left side to open the anterior leaf of the broad ligament during a da Vinci robotic hysterectomy.

The surgeon uses monopolar scissors to separate the UV fold and bladder. The right uterine artery is coagulated and cut. On the left side, the round ligament, fallopian tube, and ovarian ligament are separated. The posterior peritoneum is dissected up to the cervix at the junction with the uterosacral ligament. The left uterine artery is coagulated and cut medially. A full circle colpotomizer is inserted, and posterior colpotomy is performed following the completion of anterior colpotomy.
The uterus is extracted through the vaginal opening and temporarily placed there to prevent gas leakage. The surgeon then closes the vaginal vault using a bidirectional barbed suture.
